page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-4 is a virtual machine instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 4.0 GB RAM-per-core ratio. Powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7B13 processor, the instance allocates 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The CPU configuration includes a 32 KB L1 data cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It lacks local storage and bundled GPUs, and does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a split performance profile. The instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core benchmarks, such as stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, fall into the average and weak categories respectively. Memory bandwidth for large blocks is average, while small-block cached read-only memory bandwidth is weak. Cryptographic capabilities are supported by hardware-accelerated AES, VAES, and SHA_NI instructions, resulting in average Passmark CPU encryption performance.

Due to its weak multi-threaded decompression, database operations, and static web serving throughput, the c2d-standard-4 is less suited for heavy database hosting or high-traffic web servers. It is better aligned with general-purpose workloads, single-threaded processing tasks, or lightweight text processing. The instance offers a dedicated hardware profile with a balanced resource density, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads that benefit from dedicated AMD EPYC cores but do not require high multi-core scaling or local NVMe storage.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-4 is a virtual machine instance configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, running on an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 2 physical cores and 2 threads per core. The hardware profile lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU execution, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests. Conversely, multi-core performance, software compilation, and database operations register in the lower tiers. The instance features hardware-assisted cryptography via AES and SHA_NI flags, showing average performance in encryption tests. Given its weak multi-threaded scaling and lack of local storage, this instance is best suited for general-purpose workloads, single-threaded applications, and basic text processing tasks rather than high-throughput database or web-serving environments.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-4 features 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 16 GB RAM. It delivers strong single-core performance but exhibits weaker multi-core and database execution.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-4 is an x86_64 VM with 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, optimized for single-core workloads.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC instance delivering strong single-core performance and balanced memory for general-purpose and single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor
16.0 GB of system memory with a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Strong single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25%
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for single-threaded tasks and lightweight text processing
